chunks.text: Adequate ventilation is required to protect building users from pollutants and to remove water vapour. Bathrooms and kitchens are wet rooms requiring extract ventilation.

For bathrooms without openable windows, intermittent extract ventilation of at least 15 l/s (or 8 l/s if the fan runs continuously) is typically required. Run-on timers are recommended after shower use.
Kitchens require extract ventilation. Cooker hoods should extract to outside where possible. Minimum intermittent extract rates apply based on hob type and room location.
Part P applies to electrical installations in dwellings. Notifiable work includes new circuits, consumer unit replacement, and work in special locations such as bathrooms.
Bathrooms have zones 0, 1, and 2. Only appropriate IP-rated equipment may be installed in each zone. Electrical work in bathrooms is typically notifiable to building control.
Notifiable electrical work must be certified. This may be via a registered competent person scheme or by submitting a building notice to the local authority.

title: Approved Document H — Drainage and Waste Disposal

chunks.text: Foul water drainage must be adequately sized and vented. New connections to existing drainage may require building control approval.
Wastes from baths, showers, basins, and WCs must connect to foul drainage. Trap seals and adequate falls are required to prevent blockages and odours.

title: Approved Document G — Sanitation, Hot Water Safety and Water Efficiency

chunks.text: Hot water systems must be installed safely. Unvented hot water cylinders require notification to building control and competent person certification.
New dwellings and some renovations must meet water efficiency targets. Flow restrictors and efficient fittings may be required.
Building work must not impair the structural stability of the building. Load-bearing alterations, removals, and new openings require structural assessment.
Foundations must be suitable for ground conditions. Extensions and loft conversions typically require structural calculations and building control inspection.
Dwelling alterations must maintain adequate means of escape. Open-plan changes and loft conversions require careful fire safety design.
Fire-resistant separation is required between dwellings and between certain rooms. Penetrations through fire-rated elements must be properly sealed.

title: Approved Document L — Conservation of Fuel and Power

chunks.text: Building work must meet energy efficiency requirements. Extensions, new openings, and replacement windows must comply with U-value standards.
Easily accessible doors and windows in new dwellings must meet security standards (PAS 24). Replacement windows in extensions may need to comply.
Wet areas require a suitable waterproofing system (tanking) extending to defined heights. Building control may request manufacturer specifications and installation certificates.
Shower areas require adequate falls to waste (typically minimum 1:80). Linear drains and shower trays must be installed per manufacturer guidance.

BS 7671 sets requirements for electrical installations. RCD protection, circuit design, and testing are required. Electrical Installation Certificates (EIC) or Minor Works Certificates must be provided.
On completion, installations must be tested including continuity, insulation resistance, polarity, and RCD operation. Certificates must be issued before handover.
